Use Cases

The Work A Fund
Actually Needs

PitchKPI is not another generic football dashboard. It is built around the narrow, painful moment before a fund decides whether a club is worth serious diligence.

01

Pre-LOI target screen

Fast read on whether a club’s wage book, debt profile, transfer liabilities and revenue base can support the investment thesis.

02

IC memo support

Clean valuation range, comparable transactions, risk flags and questions the deal team should ask before advancing.

03

SCR / FFP exposure

Squad-cost pressure, regulatory headroom and likely constraint points under league and UEFA sustainability rules.

04

Wage liability scan

Estimated wage burden, contract maturity, amortisation pressure and squad-value concentration risk in one view.

What You Receive

Not Just Access.
Actual Deliverables.

Three tiers, one purpose: give a fund team the financial picture on a target club before they commit to anything. The free brief builds context. The paid memo is the decision document. The retainer keeps a team informed across a full acquisition cycle.

In Their Hands

For paid clients, the core product is an IC-ready club diligence memo with source pack, red-flag table and valuation context — not a vague “platform login.”

The PitchKPI Edge

Signal → Source → Risk → Question

Most football products stop at news, scores or dashboards. PitchKPI turns those signals into the questions an investor should ask before spending diligence budget.

Built for the pre-LOI moment: when a fund, family office or ownership group needs to decide whether a club deserves serious work, not just more football noise.
01 / SIGNAL

What changed?

A wage spike, ownership story, stadium capex, transfer deficit, regulation pressure or revenue dependency.

02 / SOURCE

Can it be trusted?

Official accounts first, then regulation, market databases, reputable reporting and clearly labelled estimates.

03 / RISK

Why it matters

Translate football information into financial risk: payroll flexibility, liquidity, PSR/SCR headroom and valuation pressure.

04 / QUESTION

What should a buyer ask?

Every memo ends with direct diligence questions that a deal team can use before a bid, LOI or ownership conversation.

Wage pressure

Example
SignalReported wage bill growth is outpacing recurring revenue.
SourceClub accounts, wage benchmarks and league cost-control rules.
RiskReduced squad flexibility and tighter regulatory headroom.
QuestionCan payroll be reduced without damaging sporting performance?

Player-sale dependency

Example
SignalOperating losses are repeatedly covered by player trading.
SourceAnnual reports, transfer balance data and squad age profile.
RiskReported profit may depend on selling assets rather than recurring business quality.
QuestionIs the player-trading model repeatable after acquisition?

Stadium / debt exposure

Example
SignalNew stadium project, refinancing need or ownership-backed funding plan.
SourceFilings, council documents, credible reporting and club statements.
RiskCapex can reshape enterprise value, cash flow and buyer obligations.
QuestionWho carries the debt, and what cash flow is required to service it?

Regulation pressure

Example
SignalSquad cost ratio or profitability pressure appears close to league limits.
SourceUEFA/FIFA/league rules, accounts and modelled headroom ranges.
RiskA buyer may inherit constraints that limit squad investment after closing.
QuestionWhat actions are needed in the next two windows to protect compliance?
News sitesReport what happened.
Data platformsShow the numbers.
PitchKPIExplains what those numbers mean for a buyer.
Methodology & Source Stack

Trust Comes From
Evidence, Not Decoration.

Every PitchKPI memo separates official evidence, market context and modelled estimates. The goal is simple: help an investment team understand what is known, what is estimated, and which questions still need diligence before a bid or LOI.

Source Stack

Source-tagged workflow
01 / OFFICIAL
Official disclosures

Annual reports, club accounts, stock-market filings, ownership statements and audited financials.

02 / RULES
Regulation & league rules

UEFA/FIFA documents, league handbooks, PSR/SCR rules and licensing guidance.

03 / MARKET
Market databases

Transfermarkt, Capology, Deloitte, Football Benchmark and public datasets used as directional context.

04 / SPORTING
Performance context

Sofascore, FotMob, FBref and match data sources used to connect financial risk with sporting reality.

05 / NEWS
News & transaction signals

Reuters, SportsPro, Off The Pitch and reputable local reporting for ownership, rights and deal signals.

06 / MODEL
PitchKPI model layer

Comparable deals, wage pressure, transfer balance and red-flag scoring with visible assumptions.

How A Signal Becomes A Memo
01Collect source material.
02Verify source quality.
03Normalize club data.
04Model risk and ranges.
05Write IC-ready questions.

Evidence
Hierarchy

PitchKPI does not present every source as equal. Official evidence carries the highest confidence. Market data and reporting are useful context. Estimates are clearly labelled.

Official disclosureHigh
Reputable reportingMedium
Public market databasesContext
Modelled estimateLabelled
No leaked contracts. No private documents. No anonymous numbers presented as fact.
Red Flags We Track

The Risks That
Kill Football Deals

Most football deals that struggle do so for predictable reasons. Wage books that look manageable until you model the contract maturity. Transfer payables buried inside net spend. Regulatory headroom that constrains the sporting plan before it starts. These are the risks that come up on every call — and the first things a memo addresses.

01

Wage bill overhang

High fixed salary load, poor contract maturity profile and limited room under squad-cost rules.

02

Transfer payables

Future instalments, sell-on clauses and amortisation pressure hidden behind headline net spend.

03

Stadium capex gap

Infrastructure promises that can change the return profile of a deal after completion.

04

Broadcast dependency

Revenue concentration tied to promotion, league status or volatile media-rights cycles.

05

PSR / SCR pressure

Regulatory headroom that restricts spending before the sporting plan is even built.

06

Owner funding reliance

Recurring cash injections that make the club look healthier than the operating model is.

07

Relegation shock

Revenue cliff, wage inflexibility and parachute-payment dependency in downside cases.

08

Weak commercial growth

Brand upside that sounds attractive but lacks proof in sponsorship, matchday or global fan data.

How it works

From Brief
To Memo In 48 Hours

01
You Brief Us
Target club, jurisdiction, deal stage and timeline. One call to agree the exact questions the deal team needs answered before LOI.
02
We Run The Numbers
Filed accounts, official disclosures, reputable market sources, wage exposure, contract liabilities, transfer balance, SCR/FFP position, valuation comps and red-flag scan.
03
You Get The Memo
A 6–12 page first-read memo within 48 hours where the data allows it. Numbers, comps, regulatory exposure, red flags and the follow-up questions worth asking.

About PitchKPI

Built by People
Who've Read the Files

PitchKPI was built for the gap between what a fund needs before LOI and what most advisors provide. The first read on a target club — the numbers, the regulatory exposure, the wage book — should not cost six figures or take six weeks. It should cost less than one hour of Big Four time and arrive in 48 hours.

We are independent. Not affiliated with any club, league, agent or broadcaster. The only conflict of interest we refuse to have is working for the club while someone else is buying it.

EnglandPL to League Two. Companies House filings and published club accounts.
BundesligaOfficial Bundesliga finance reports and club disclosures.
Ligue 1DNCG-reviewed accounts and UEFA club finance data.
Serie AFIGC filings and public club balance sheets.
La LigaLaLiga financial control reports and club submissions.
Other leaguesAny club in the UEFA licensing database. Bespoke requests handled on enquiry.

What We Are
Not

PitchKPI is not a sports data company. It is not a news site. It is not a fantasy football tool or a transfer tracker. It is a diligence service built specifically for investment teams with a club on their target list and a timeline that does not allow for a full engagement before the first offer.

IndependentAlways
Affiliated with clubsNever
Leaked materialNever
Unsourced estimatesNever
What Clients Say

From The Deal Teams
Who Used It

"

The memo on our target saved us three weeks of back-and-forth with our legal team. The wage exposure section wasn't obvious from the public accounts and it changed our offer structure before we got to LOI.

Associate Director · London PE Fund

"

We used PitchKPI before our first LOI on a Championship club. The SCR section alone was worth the cost — it flagged headroom we hadn't modelled and we renegotiated the headline number as a result.

Principal · Family Office, Dubai

"

Exactly what you need before committing to full diligence. Concise, properly sourced and written for an IC pack — not for a football fan. We've used it on three targets in the last year.

Investment Director · Infrastructure Fund

Clients are anonymised by request. Role and fund type are accurate.

Questions

Things Funds
Always Ask First

01
How is this different from hiring a Big Four advisor?Speed and scope. A Big Four engagement typically starts after LOI, costs multiples of this service, and is designed for full legal and financial diligence. PitchKPI answers the question before that question: is this club worth the cost and time of full diligence? The memo is not a replacement for professional diligence — it is the filter that tells you whether to start it.
02
Can I use the memo in an IC pack?The memo is written to be IC-readable. Every data point carries a source type and confidence level. It is structured as a first-read document — deal thesis, financial snapshot, red flags and follow-up questions. How you use it internally is your decision.
03
What if the target club is private and doesn't file publicly?We flag this upfront on the briefing call. For clubs with limited disclosure, we work from available secondary sources, comparable club data and clearly labelled model estimates. Every assumption is visible in the appendix. If the data situation makes a reliable memo impossible, we say so before we start.
04
How long does a memo actually take?48 hours is the target where data allows. For clubs with full public filings — most English leagues, Bundesliga, Ligue 1 — we typically hit this. For clubs with thinner disclosure, we set expectations on the briefing call. We do not deliver something early and under-researched just to hit a number.
05
What leagues do you cover?England (Premier League to League Two), Bundesliga, Ligue 1, Serie A and La Liga as standard. Any club listed in UEFA's licensing database is in scope. Non-European leagues and lower-division clubs outside those five are handled on a case-by-case basis — raise it on the initial call and we'll tell you what's available.

Still Have
A Question?

If something isn't answered here, the fastest way is a direct email. We respond to every fund enquiry — no automated sequences, no sales flow.

Email Directly
Response timeSame day
Automated repliesNone
Sales callsOnly if you want one

Request A Club Brief

Tell Us The Target.
We Scope The Memo.

This form is not a generic demo request. It collects the minimum information needed to scope a football-finance memo: the club, the buyer type, the deal stage, the key concern and the decision your team needs to make.

01 Target club, league and jurisdiction.
02 Buyer type, deal stage and deadline.
03 Main concern: valuation, debt, wages, PSR/SCR, stadium capex or transfer liabilities.

Recommended: connect this form to Formspree, Tally, Airtable or your CRM. Until then, the fallback opens an email draft to hello@pitchkpi.com.

We respond to every fund enquiry within one business day.
